edge: WSI classification is a crucial component in the broader process of automated pathology analysis.
Most proposed methods for WSI classification follow the MIL formulation due to high-resolution challenges and lack of patch-level labels.
MIL is prone to learn spurious associations that can negatively impact the model's performance.
The proposed method develops a causal MIL framework to address the issue of spurious associations in WSI classification.
The framework uses the front-door adjustment to block spurious associations between features and predictions.
The proposed method is evaluated on two publicly available WSI datasets: Camelyon16 and TCGA-NSCLC.
Camelyon16 is a dataset used for evaluating the performance of the causal MIL framework.
TCGA-NSCLC is another dataset used for evaluating the proposed method
The framework is designed to seamlessly integrate with various feature extractors and aggregators.
